rationale: The determinism check requires post1_packets@ == post2_packets@ (Seq equality), but the function's postcondition only constrains post1_packets@.map_values(|p: CPacket| p@).to_set() to equal pre_packets + un_acked_messages_for_dest set. Two Vec<CPacket> with the same map_values().to_set() can be different sequences (different element ordering, different multiplicities, different underlying CPacket values whose views collapse to same Packet). The to_set equality between post1 and post2 was proven via assert ... =~=, but Seq equality is not derivable from set equality. Attempted: direct assertion, assertion-by-implication with antecedent unfolding. All failed at the post1_packets@ =~= post2_packets@ step. Without strengthening the function spec or introducing axioms (forbidden), this determinism claim is logically unsound at the Seq level.
